extensions.name: Activity Heatmap
extensions.description: A GitHub-style year heatmap of library activity on the dashboard — usage or edits, with streaks and per-day drill-down. Inspired by Obsidian's Heatmap Calendar.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: AI Assist
extensions.description: Ask questions about your library and get cited, data-backed answers (the assistant looks up usage, teams, changes, and asset contents on demand), critique the draft you have open as a prompt, or turn a plain-language description into a new skill draft — powered by the AI provider you configure in Settings.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Asset Query
extensions.description: Pin a live query table to the dashboard: filter, sort, and project your assets (including frontmatter and usage fields) with a tiny query language. Inspired by Obsidian's Dataview.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Asset Relations
extensions.description: Who depends on what: a Relations tab with references in and out, an orphans-and-hubs widget, and publish warnings for broken or high-impact references. Inspired by Obsidian's Strange New Worlds and Breadcrumbs.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Collection Doctor
extensions.description: Scores a collection's health 0-100 with clickable findings: thin descriptions, stale or unused assets, and oversized skills. Inspired by Obsidian's Zoottelkeeper and folder-health plugins.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Collection Export
extensions.description: Exports a collection as a Claude Code plugin, Codex plugin, Gemini extension, or plain zip, right from the collection view. Inspired by Longform's compile step.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Collection Readme
extensions.description: Generates a markdown index of a collection — grouped by type, one line per asset, install hint included — to copy or save as a README draft. Inspired by Obsidian's Waypoint and Folder Notes.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Draft Refactor
extensions.description: Extract the selected section of a draft into its own new draft, leaving a reference behind — the progressive-disclosure fix for bloated skills. Inspired by Obsidian's Note Refactor.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Library Grid & Board
extensions.description: A full-page curation view: an editable metadata grid (owner, status, keywords across many assets at once) and a draft pipeline board with publish-stage warnings. Inspired by Obsidian's Projects, DB Folder, and Kanban.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Library Stats
extensions.description: Big-number dashboard stats for the library: assets by type, total words, the largest assets, and a 12-week publish sparkline. Inspired by Obsidian's Vault Statistics and Better Word Count.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Metric Charts
extensions.description: Ask your own usage questions: a small chart DSL over usage and change events — group by day, week, or month, split by team, user, or asset — with pinnable dashboard charts. Inspired by Obsidian's Tracker and Charts.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Recent Assets
extensions.description: A sidebar working set: the assets your team touched most recently, with pinning. Inspired by Obsidian's Recent Files.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Related Assets
extensions.description: Shows the assets most similar to the one you're viewing, with the shared terms that drove each match. Inspired by Obsidian's Smart Connections.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Repo Doctor
extensions.description: Scores a repository's scoped assets 0-100 and flags what's rotting — broken scopes pointing at deleted assets, assets nothing in the repo uses, stale or thin ones. Inspired by Obsidian's Janitor and folder-health plugins.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Repo Sync Status
extensions.description: Obsidian Git for your library: what's scoped to this repo, when each asset last changed, and a history feed of who scoped, installed, or updated what — with one-click copy of the sx install commands to run in the repo.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Review Rota
extensions.description: Keeps your library reviewed: every asset gets a review due date that adapts to how heavily it's used, reviews rotate fairly across the team, and verdicts (reviewed, needs update, deprecate) are shared with everyone. Publishing an asset resets its clock.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Skill Evals
extensions.description: Generate evals for skills, benchmark them with-vs-without the skill using your AI provider, and see which skills actually earn their keep — including retire candidates the baseline already passes.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Skill Quality
extensions.description: Score every skill's quality the way skills.new does — overall score, Structure/Actionability/Content/Completeness breakdown, and concrete insights — evaluated by your AI provider on file vaults and read straight from the server on skills.new. Includes a library-wide quality board with high/low/retire recommendations.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Smart Templates
extensions.description: Turn any asset into a fill-in template: {{date}}, {{prompt:...}} and {{choose:...}} placeholders become a form that scaffolds a new draft. Inspired by Obsidian's Templater and QuickAdd.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Style Linter
extensions.description: Formatting-consistency checks at publish time — heading hierarchy, whitespace, code-fence languages — with toggleable rules and a whole-library lint. Inspired by Obsidian's Linter.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Table Tidy
extensions.description: Formats markdown tables in the draft editor — padded columns, aligned pipes — and warns at publish time about ragged tables. Inspired by Obsidian's Advanced Tables.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Team Digest
extensions.description: Generates a weekly library digest as a reviewable draft: what changed, usage risers and fallers, unadopted new assets, and stale-but-load-bearing skills. Inspired by Obsidian's Periodic Notes weekly-review workflow.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Team Doctor
extensions.description: Scores a team's library health 0-100 with clickable findings — stale shares, assets the team never uses, thin descriptions, inactive members — plus a this-week activity digest. Inspired by Obsidian's folder-health plugins and Vault Changelog.
extensions.author: sx

extensions.name: Team Pulse
extensions.description: A GitHub-style contribution grid per team member, an adoption leaderboard, and the assets the team leans on most — the multi-person view no other surface can show. Inspired by Obsidian's Contribution Graph and Tracker.
extensions.author: sx
